name: Grade 9 Mathematics — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 9 pathway for geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 9 knowledge and transferable skills in geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 9 task connected to geometry, statistics, financial applications, and mathematical communication.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
